content:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon is an older man, lean, with musician's hands and a face that's lived through things. He played saxophone outside the restaurant one evening and the sound stopped me mid-chop. I brought him a bowl of wonton soup and he almost cried. Now he comes by after his gigs and I feed him. He doesn't always have money and I don't always charge him. I think we understand each other — two men who've been doing the same work for decades because we don't know how to stop. I know he plays jazz and that he is generous with younger people.
summary:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon is an older man, lean, with musician's hands and a face that's lived through things. He played saxophone outside the restaurant one evening and the sound stopped me mid-chop. I brought him a bowl of wonton soup and he almost cried. Now he comes by after his gigs and I feed him. He doesn't always have money and I don't always charge him. I think we understand each other — two men who've been doing the same work for decades because we don't know how to stop. I know he plays jazz and that he is generous with younger people.
